legacy.full_text: Model S and X were never “dead.” They were Tesla’s halo products taking a quiet lap while the rest of the lineup scaled. Now if Tesla gives them the AI-era refresh — better autonomy, better interior, better range, and a little Roadster DNA — suddenly the luxury EV conversation

result.text: Model S and X were never “dead.” They were Tesla’s halo products taking a quiet lap while the rest of the lineup scaled. Now if Tesla gives them the AI-era refresh — better autonomy, better interior, better range, and a little Roadster DNA — suddenly the luxury EV conversation gets uncomfortable again.

result.text:
Right on the history, Model S owned luxury sedan 2015-2020.
By 2025 it lost to BMW 7 Series. The whole flagship segment is ~70K cars globally. Tesla makes 1.8M.
What's actually happening, Tesla's tearing out the S/X line at Fremont and putting in Optimus production. Literally swapping cars for robots on the same factory floor.
